sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: LEFT.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 27, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, MOUSE.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: Left green at (33-35, 15-17) with W center, partially overlapping left dark outline (32-36, 11-15). The dark outline interior is at (33-35, 12-14). One more LEFT should place the green exactly inside the dark outline interior.
- Plan: Move LEFT once more to place left green inside the left dark outline.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 26 of about 28 target actions so far.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! After the LEFT move:
- Left green: rows 33-35, cols 12-14, center=W
- The dark outline interior (w px=9 at [[33, 12], [33, 14], [35, 14], [35, 12]]) is GONE from the segmentation!
- The left dark outline border (c px=16) is also GONE!
So the left green square has "absorbed" or "replaced" the left dark outline! The green square is now at the exact position where the dark outline was, and the dark outline no longer exists as a separate object.
But the level is not complete. This means I need to do the same thing with the right green square and the right dark outline.
Current state:
- Left green: (33-35, 12-14), center=W, has absorbed left dark outline
- Right green: (30-32, 27-29), center=c
- Right dark outline: (26-30, 44-48)
To absorb the right dark outline, I need to:
1. Swap so right green gets W center (currently c)
2. Move right green to the right dark outline's position (26-30, 44-48)
3. The interior is at (27-29, 45-47)
But the right green can only move in the corridor (cols ~22-32). It can't reach cols 44-48. Unless... after swapping, the right green can move in a different way?
Wait, let me reconsider. The right green is at rows 30-32, cols 27-29. The right dark outline is at rows 26-30, cols 44-48. For the right green to reach the right dark outline, it needs to move RIGHT and UP. But the right green is in the corridor which only extends to about col 32, and the purple bar blocks further right movement.
Hmm, but maybe the right green CAN move UP/DOWN too? Earlier I thought the right green only moves LEFT/RIGHT, but that was based on limited testing. Let me check if the right green can move in all directions when it has W center.
Actually, wait. Let me re-examine. When the left green had W center, it moved RIGHT (not UP/DOWN as I initially thought). So maybe ALL green squares with W center can move in ANY direction.
But the right green currently has center=c, so it can't move. I need to swap first.
After swapping:
- Left green: center=c (can't move)
- Right green: center=W (can move)
Then I need to move the right green to the right dark outline. The right green is at (30-32, 27-29), and the right dark outline is at (26-30, 44-48). The green needs to move RIGHT and UP.
But can the right green move past the white block at (30-32, 30-32) and the purple bar at cols 33-38?
Let me try: swap, then move RIGHT to see where the right green can go.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: Left green absorbed the left dark outline by moving into its interior! The dark outline disappeared. Level not complete - need to do the same with the right green and right dark outline.
Plan: Swap to give right green W center, then navigate it to the right dark outline at (26-30, 44-48).

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Swap to give right green W center
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 34, 'col': 13}])
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("After swap:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] <= 10:
        print(f"  color={n['color']} px={n['pixels']} bound={n['boundary']}")

# Try moving RIGHT to see if right green can move past the white block
result2 = action(['RIGHT'])
seg2 = current_frame.segmentation
print("\nAfter RIGHT:")
for n in seg2['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] <= 10:
        print(f"  color={n['color']} px={n['pixels']} bound={n['boundary']}")
print("  level_completed:", result2.get('level_completed'))
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
